title: Carbeth Plants Astrantia Rubra Bare Root - Dark Red Masterwort Hardy Shrub - Pollinator Friendly Long Flowering Perennial for Shaded Cottage and Woodland Gardens (1, Rubra)
features: UNIQUE STAR-SHAPED BLOOMS: Produces intricate clusters of starry flowers above finely cut foliage, adding elegance and texture to garden beds and borders.
BARE ROOT PERENNIAL: Supplied dormant and soil-free, Astrantia bare roots establish quickly in the garden, rewarding you with reliable growth and repeat flowering.
LONG FLOWERING SEASON: Blooms from early to late summer, providing months of continuous colour and interest in your outdoor space.
POLLINATOR FRIENDLY: Nectar-rich flowers are highly attractive to bees, butterflies, and other beneficial insects, perfect for wildlife gardens.
EASY TO GROW: Hardy and low maintenance, Astrantia thrives in partial shade with moist, well-drained soil and returns each year stronger than before.
description: Product Description Bare Root Bestsellers Bare Root Wallflowers Buying Options Agapanthus Bare Roots Buying Options Eryngium Bare Roots Buying Options Peony Bare Roots Buying Options Erigeron Bare Roots Buying Options Customer Reviews 3.2 out of 5 stars 35 — 4.0 out of 5 stars 1 4.0 out of 5 stars 21 — Quantity 10 Bare Roots 1-9 Bare Roots 1-9 Bare Roots 1-3 Bare Roots 1-9 Bare Roots Light Requirement Full Sun to Partial Shade Full Sun Full sun Full Sun to Partial Shade Full Sun Colour Multicolour White, Blue Blue, Silver Multicolour Pink, White Eventual Height Up to 45cm Up to 150cm Up to 100cm Up to 90cm Up to 40cm Flowering Season Spring Summer - Early Autumn Summer Spring - Early Summer Summer - Autumn What are bare root perennials? Bare root perennials are plants supplied without soil, with their roots cleaned and usually wrapped to keep them moist. They are shipped in a dormant state, ready for planting in the ground or pots. When should I plant bare root perennials? The best time is during the dormant season from autumn to early spring (October–March) when the ground is workable and frost free. How long will it take for bare root perennials to start growing? Growth usually begins in spring as the soil warms. Most bare roots produce new shoots within a few weeks of planting. How do I prepare bare root perennials before planting? Soak the roots in water for 3-6 hours before planting. This rehydrates them and helps them establish more quickly. Can I plant bare root perennials in pots? Yes, they can be started in pots or containers and then transferred into the garden once they are actively growing. Cost Efficient Bare-root plants are typically cheaper than full sized, potted plants, making them the economical choice for larger garden projects Vigorous First-Year Growth Bare roots tend to produce bigger, stronger plants in their first year compared to those started from seed or cuttings Enviromentally Friendly These require no plastic pots to produce or dispose of on arrival! Easy to Plant Bare roots are easy to handle and simple to plant, making your garden displays simpler than ever. No Soil? No Problem Bareroot plants are supplied bare without soil, which makes them easier to handle, more eco-friendly, and more cost efficient!
